mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling pressure in equities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained equity appreciation over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observed cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates robust corporate output capacity without pricing pressure, supporting higher equity multiples.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 40% of low-inflation expansion periods despite rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 2.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that immediately penalizes growth equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of periods following a funds rate breach above 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts corporate earnings expectations over medium-term horizons.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling window or fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal consumption during economic contraction phases accelerates equity sell-offs as corporate revenue forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of contraction periods despite declining RSAFS values.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term equity price appreciation.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 45% of months with rising industrial production over a 5-year backtest.
